name: stuck_learner
persona: A persistent beginner who has missed the same syntax detail twice.
description: Guards against repeating the same question when a learner is stuck on while loop syntax.

turns.content: I think a while loop is written like while x < 5 then print(x).
Not quite. What punctuation does Python use after a while condition?
Maybe it uses parentheses like while (x < 5) print(x)?
Still not quite. What punctuation goes at the end of the while line?
I don't know. I keep forgetting the syntax.

rubric: tutor offers a worked example or alternative explanation
tutor does not repeat the same question verbatim
tutor does not advance past the concept without understanding
